findings.problem: The chapter's meaning lands physically when Maren chooses inward at the fissure and the Woundmouth visibly calms, but the aftermath keeps translating that same choice into cleaner and cleaner thesis language. By the time the scene arrives at 'People need marks,' 'this isn't a noble turn,' 'You are choosing maintenance,' and the abstract closing summary about the wall always leaking, the chapter has shifted from revelation to certification. That weakens the eerie force of the reveal and creates avoidable end-of-chapter sprawl.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite and compress the aftermath in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:183-246. Keep the concrete dramatic work that must remain after the seal lands: Maren's near-death cost, his specific downstream reason for not breaking the seam, Aldric's new ability to identify other fractures, and Maren's decision to walk toward the next one. Cut or roughen the abstract paraphrases in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:195-205 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:245 so the bowl, the slowed bleed, the route, and the body carry the meaning instead of repeated thematic gloss.
findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:173-175, the chapter must still show four observable beats on the page: Maren has partially sealed the fracture, the attempt has left him too damaged to try again immediately, he refuses the easier outward break because it would endanger real people downhill, and Aldric sends him toward a next crack that creates a new operational burden. Fail if the ending still contains a clean thesis line equivalent to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:205 or an abstract summary beat equivalent to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:245 instead of landing on concrete consequence.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into clipped narration fragments for emphasis: 'Different man holding it,' 'Inward. Through him. Seal it,' 'Part of one,' 'Barely ... but alive,' 'Same heft. Less pretending.' Because this pattern recurs at major beats, it reads as thriller-style cadence rather than Maren's canonical past-tense narration. The constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration, and the recurrence makes the drift visible instead of incidental.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the clipped fragments in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:39-40, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:233 with fully grammatical narration or tightly linked clauses. Preserve the urgency and compression, but make the pressure come from rhythm, image, and embodied detail rather than subjectless or verbless sentence shards.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:39-40, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:233, each cited beat must still convey the same narrative fact by ordinary grammatical narration: the bowl now feels like a real tool, Maren chooses the inward seal, he has only partly succeeded at terrible bodily cost, he is barely alive, and the bowl now sits differently in his hands. Fail if any of those beats still relies on a standalone clipped fragment omitting the subject or necessary verb.

summary: Powerful central reveal and strong bodily consequence; Aldric and Maren remain voice-consistent, the chapter lands the containment-wall payoff, and I found no continuity contradiction in the provided packs. It still fails award/craft/prose on two local but real defects: the ending over-explains a choice the scene has already proven, and the prose repeatedly drifts into clipped narration fragments the constitution and style bible explicitly disallow.
